title: Plan a Stage 8 Mathematics Exhibition
overview: Bring together everything from Stage 8 Mathematics to design a mathematics exhibition that teaches visitors the most important ideas from this stage.

project.drivingQuestion: How can we use the mathematics from Stage 8 to plan a clear, accurate and welcoming exhibition for our school or community?
project.finalProduct: Create an exhibition plan with labelled displays, a schedule, a budget, a visitor survey with a data display, and a short explanation of your mathematical decisions.

stages.title: 1. Term 1 foundations display
stages.prompt: Choose the two most important ideas from Term 1 (Units 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6). Build a display that teaches each idea with a model, an example and a check question.
stages.evidence: Two labelled displays with models, examples and check questions

stages.title: 2. Term 2 applications corner
stages.prompt: Design an activity corner where visitors use Term 2 skills (Units 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12) to solve one real problem, showing every step of the method.
stages.evidence: A worked real problem with each method step shown

stages.title: 3. Term 3 connections wall
stages.prompt: Create a connections wall that links Term 3 ideas (Units 13, 14, 15, 16) to earlier units, with at least three labelled connections.
stages.evidence: A connections wall with three labelled links between units

stages.title: 4. Present and explain
stages.prompt: Present your exhibition plan. Explain at least three mathematical choices, check that your answers are sensible and identify one improvement you would make.
stages.evidence: Spoken, written or recorded mathematical explanation

evidenceChecklist: A worked real problem with method steps
A connections wall with three links
A schedule and budget for the exhibition
A survey with organised data display

rubric.secure: Calculations, measures, shapes, data and models are accurate and checked.
The plan connects ideas from several Stage 8 units for a clear purpose.
Labels, diagrams, tables or charts make the mathematics visible.
Decisions are explained using appropriate mathematical words and evidence.

questions.question: What does “Integer” mean?
options: A whole number: positive, negative or zero
Distance from zero; never negative
The number multiplied / how many times
Exactly two factors: 1 and itself
questions.answer: A whole number: positive, negative or zero
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Integer means A whole number: positive, negative or zero.

questions.question: What does “Absolute value” mean?
options: Distance from zero; never negative
The number multiplied / how many times
Exactly two factors: 1 and itself
A number written as a product of primes
questions.answer: Distance from zero; never negative
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Absolute value means Distance from zero; never negative.

questions.question: What does “Variable” mean?
options: A letter standing for an unknown or changing number
A single part of an expression, separated by + or −
The number multiplying a variable
A term with no variable
questions.answer: A letter standing for an unknown or changing number
questions.hint: Use the Unit 2 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Variable means A letter standing for an unknown or changing number.

questions.question: What does “Term” mean?
options: A single part of an expression, separated by + or −
The number multiplying a variable
A term with no variable
Terms with the same variable part
questions.answer: A single part of an expression, separated by + or −
questions.hint: Use the Unit 2 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Term means A single part of an expression, separated by + or −.

questions.question: What does “Place value” mean?
options: The value a digit has from its position
10 raised to a whole-number exponent, such as 10³ = 1000
A position to the right of the decimal point
A digit counted from the first non-zero digit
questions.answer: The value a digit has from its position
questions.hint: Use the Unit 3 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Place value means The value a digit has from its position.

questions.question: What does “Power of 10” mean?
options: 10 raised to a whole-number exponent, such as 10³ = 1000
A position to the right of the decimal point
A digit counted from the first non-zero digit
a × 10ⁿ with 1 ≤ a < 10
questions.answer: 10 raised to a whole-number exponent, such as 10³ = 1000
questions.hint: Use the Unit 3 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Power of 10 means 10 raised to a whole-number exponent, such as 10³ = 1000.

questions.question: What does “Decimal point” mean?
options: Dot separating whole numbers from parts
The first three places after the point
How many digits come after the point
A zero at the end that does not change value
questions.answer: Dot separating whole numbers from parts
questions.hint: Use the Unit 4 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Decimal point means Dot separating whole numbers from parts.

questions.question: What does “Tenths / Hundredths / Thousandths” mean?
options: The first three places after the point
How many digits come after the point
A zero at the end that does not change value
A decimal that stops, like 0.75
questions.answer: The first three places after the point
questions.hint: Use the Unit 4 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Tenths / Hundredths / Thousandths means The first three places after the point.

questions.question: What does “Transversal” mean?
options: A line crossing two or more other lines
Same position; equal (F-shape)
Opposite sides, between the lines; equal (Z-shape)
Same side, between the lines; add to 180° (C-shape)
questions.answer: A line crossing two or more other lines
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Transversal means A line crossing two or more other lines.

questions.question: What does “Corresponding angles” mean?
options: Same position; equal (F-shape)
Opposite sides, between the lines; equal (Z-shape)
Same side, between the lines; add to 180° (C-shape)
Equal angles where two lines cross
questions.answer: Same position; equal (F-shape)
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles means Same position; equal (F-shape).

questions.question: What does “Quantitative / Qualitative” mean?
options: Number data / category data
Counted whole values / measured any value
No order / natural order
Whole group / representative part
questions.answer: Number data / category data
questions.hint: Use the Unit 6 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Quantitative / Qualitative means Number data / category data.

questions.question: What does “Discrete / Continuous” mean?
options: Counted whole values / measured any value
No order / natural order
Whole group / representative part
Data from every member of the population
questions.answer: Counted whole values / measured any value
questions.hint: Use the Unit 6 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Discrete / Continuous means Counted whole values / measured any value.

questions.question: What does “Numerator / Denominator” mean?
options: Top number / bottom number of a fraction
Value less than 1 / value 1 or more
A whole number plus a proper fraction
Different fractions with the same value
questions.answer: Top number / bottom number of a fraction
questions.hint: Use the Unit 7 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Numerator / Denominator means Top number / bottom number of a fraction.

questions.question: What does “Proper / Improper” mean?
options: Value less than 1 / value 1 or more
A whole number plus a proper fraction
Different fractions with the same value
No common factor except 1
questions.answer: Value less than 1 / value 1 or more
questions.hint: Use the Unit 7 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Proper / Improper means Value less than 1 / value 1 or more.

questions.question: What does “Line of symmetry” mean?
options: A mirror line splitting a shape into matching halves
How many times a shape matches itself in one full turn
All sides and all angles equal
Angle inside a polygon / angle to the extended side
questions.answer: A mirror line splitting a shape into matching halves
questions.hint: Use the Unit 8 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Line of symmetry means A mirror line splitting a shape into matching halves.

questions.question: What does “Order of rotational symmetry” mean?
options: How many times a shape matches itself in one full turn
All sides and all angles equal
Angle inside a polygon / angle to the extended side
Corner / line where faces meet / flat surface
questions.answer: How many times a shape matches itself in one full turn
questions.hint: Use the Unit 8 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Order of rotational symmetry means How many times a shape matches itself in one full turn.

questions.question: What does “Sequence” mean?
options: An ordered list of numbers following a rule
A single number in a sequence
The fixed step in a linear sequence
A formula giving any term from its position n
questions.answer: An ordered list of numbers following a rule
questions.hint: Use the Unit 9 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Sequence means An ordered list of numbers following a rule.

questions.question: What does “Term” mean?
options: A single number in a sequence
The fixed step in a linear sequence
A formula giving any term from its position n
A rule giving one output for each input
questions.answer: A single number in a sequence
questions.hint: Use the Unit 9 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Term means A single number in a sequence.

questions.question: What does “Percentage” mean?
options: A part of something out of 100
The decimal you multiply by to make a change
The value before any change
Finding the original from the final amount
questions.answer: A part of something out of 100
questions.hint: Use the Unit 10 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Percentage means A part of something out of 100.

questions.question: What does “Multiplier” mean?
options: The decimal you multiply by to make a change
The value before any change
Finding the original from the final amount
The starting amount saved or borrowed
questions.answer: The decimal you multiply by to make a change
questions.hint: Use the Unit 10 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Multiplier means The decimal you multiply by to make a change.

questions.question: What does “Coordinates” mean?
options: A pair (x, y) fixing a point's position
Steepness of a line: rise ÷ run
Where a line crosses the y-axis (x = 0)
Where a line crosses the x-axis (y = 0)
questions.answer: A pair (x, y) fixing a point's position
questions.hint: Use the Unit 11 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Coordinates means A pair (x, y) fixing a point's position.

questions.question: What does “Gradient (m)” mean?
options: Steepness of a line: rise ÷ run
Where a line crosses the y-axis (x = 0)
Where a line crosses the x-axis (y = 0)
A graph that is a straight line
questions.answer: Steepness of a line: rise ÷ run
questions.hint: Use the Unit 11 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Gradient (m) means Steepness of a line: rise ÷ run.

questions.question: What does “Ratio” mean?
options: A comparison of two or more quantities
Divide every part by the HCF to lowest terms
Different ratios that describe the same relationship
A statement that two ratios are equal
questions.answer: A comparison of two or more quantities
questions.hint: Use the Unit 12 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Ratio means A comparison of two or more quantities.

questions.question: What does “Simplify” mean?
options: Divide every part by the HCF to lowest terms
Different ratios that describe the same relationship
A statement that two ratios are equal
As one quantity rises, the other rises at the same rate
questions.answer: Divide every part by the HCF to lowest terms
questions.hint: Use the Unit 12 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Simplify means Divide every part by the HCF to lowest terms.

questions.question: What does “Outcome” mean?
options: A single possible result of a trial
The set of all possible outcomes
An outcome or group of outcomes you want
The event 'not A'; P(not A) = 1 − P(A)
questions.answer: A single possible result of a trial
questions.hint: Use the Unit 13 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Outcome means A single possible result of a trial.

questions.question: What does “Sample space” mean?
options: The set of all possible outcomes
An outcome or group of outcomes you want
The event 'not A'; P(not A) = 1 − P(A)
Events that cannot both happen at once
questions.answer: The set of all possible outcomes
questions.hint: Use the Unit 13 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Sample space means The set of all possible outcomes.

questions.question: What does “Origin” mean?
options: The point (0, 0) where the axes cross
One of the four regions of the plane
The shape before / after a transformation
(a, b): horizontal then vertical shift
questions.answer: The point (0, 0) where the axes cross
questions.hint: Use the Unit 14 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Origin means The point (0, 0) where the axes cross.

questions.question: What does “Quadrant” mean?
options: One of the four regions of the plane
The shape before / after a transformation
(a, b): horizontal then vertical shift
The line a shape is reflected across
questions.answer: One of the four regions of the plane
questions.hint: Use the Unit 14 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Quadrant means One of the four regions of the plane.

questions.question: What does “Area” mean?
options: Flat surface a 2D shape covers (square units)
Total distance around a shape
The perimeter of a circle
Centre to edge / across through centre (d = 2r)
questions.answer: Flat surface a 2D shape covers (square units)
questions.hint: Use the Unit 15 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Area means Flat surface a 2D shape covers (square units).

questions.question: What does “Perimeter” mean?
options: Total distance around a shape
The perimeter of a circle
Centre to edge / across through centre (d = 2r)
Circumference ÷ diameter ≈ 3.14
questions.answer: Total distance around a shape
questions.hint: Use the Unit 15 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Perimeter means Total distance around a shape.

questions.question: What does “Mean / Median / Mode” mean?
options: The three averages: total÷n / middle / most frequent
Highest minus lowest value
Values splitting ordered data into quarters
Q3 − Q1; spread of the middle 50%
questions.answer: The three averages: total÷n / middle / most frequent
questions.hint: Use the Unit 16 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Mean / Median / Mode means The three averages: total÷n / middle / most frequent.

questions.question: What does “Range” mean?
options: Highest minus lowest value
Values splitting ordered data into quarters
Q3 − Q1; spread of the middle 50%
How a set of values is spread out
questions.answer: Highest minus lowest value
questions.hint: Use the Unit 16 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Range means Highest minus lowest value.
